name: ASUS Zenbook S14 (2025)
shortDescription: The ASUS Zenbook S14 is the best Intel Copilot+ laptop — Intel Core Ultra 7 Series 2, 47 TOPS NPU, 32GB RAM, 3K OLED 120Hz touch display, and Thunderbolt 4. Best-in-class display with the most RAM of any NPU laptop in this roundup.
useCases: Running 13B models locally — 32GB RAM is sufficient for 13B Q4 with headroom
On-device AI with Intel OpenVINO and ONNX Runtime NPU acceleration
High-quality AI-assisted creative work on a 3K OLED display
Developer workstation with Thunderbolt 4 dock support
Copilot+ Windows features via Intel NPU
pros: 32GB RAM — the highest of any NPU laptop here, enables 13B Q4 local inference
47 TOPS NPU — slightly more than Snapdragon X Elite's 45 TOPS
3K OLED 120Hz touch display — best screen in the NPU laptop category
Thunderbolt 4 — docks, eGPUs, and fast external storage all supported
Full x64 native performance — no ARM emulation compatibility issues
cons: Intel Arc integrated GPU is less efficient than Snapdragon X Elite GPU for AI
Higher power consumption than ARM-based Snapdragon X Elite — shorter battery life
Intel NPU ecosystem (OpenVINO) less user-friendly than Qualcomm's for beginners
verdict: The ASUS Zenbook S14 is the best Intel Copilot+ laptop and the only one in this roundup with 32GB RAM — a meaningful advantage for running 13B models locally. If you need x64 compatibility, a stunning display, and Thunderbolt 4, this beats the Surface Laptop 7 for developer use. Battery life is shorter than Snapdragon-based laptops, but the performance and display quality justify the trade-off for desk-forward workflows.

faq.question: Can the ASUS Zenbook S14 run a 13B LLM locally?
faq.answer: Yes. With 32GB unified memory, the Zenbook S14 loads Llama 2 13B at Q4_K_M (requires ~8GB) entirely in RAM with significant headroom. CPU inference via llama.cpp delivers 3–6 tokens/sec. Intel Arc's iGPU with OpenVINO can accelerate inference on compatible models to 8–15 tokens/sec — slower than dedicated GPU but usable for local chat.

faq.question: How does Intel's 47 TOPS NPU compare to Qualcomm's 45 TOPS?
faq.answer: Raw TOPS numbers are not directly comparable between Intel and Qualcomm because they measure different operation precisions. In practice, Qualcomm's NPU has broader current software support via QNN (Qualcomm Neural Network SDK). Intel's NPU uses OpenVINO and ONNX Runtime and is improving rapidly. For most Windows AI Studio and Copilot+ features, both perform comparably.

faq.question: Does Thunderbolt 4 matter for AI on the Zenbook S14?
faq.answer: Yes. Thunderbolt 4 enables connecting a TB4 dock (like the CalDigit TS4 or Anker 777) for multi-monitor setups, and more importantly enables external GPU enclosures via Thunderbolt — though eGPU performance on TB4 is bandwidth-limited compared to native PCIe. TB4 also enables fast external NVMe access for storing large model libraries.

notFor: All-day mobile use — battery life is shorter than Snapdragon X Elite laptops
Users needing ARM-native AI tool support — some Qualcomm-optimized models won't run on Intel NPU
entityHeadline: ASUS Zenbook S14 Review: Best Intel Copilot+ Laptop — 32GB RAM, 3K OLED, 47 TOPS NPU
